programs.name: Senior CommUnity Care of Kentucky (PACE)

eligibility.income_limits: No specific income limits or dollar amounts listed; dually eligible (Medicare and Medicaid) receive 100% coverage, while Medicare-only participants pay a fee and private pay options exist with different structures[1][2][3][5]
eligibility.asset_limits: No asset limits or exemptions specified in available data[1][2][3][5]
other_requirements: Certified by the state to need nursing home level of care (per 907 KAR 1:022)[2][3][5]
Able to live safely in the community at time of enrollment with PACE services[1][2][3]
Reside in a PACE service area (Jefferson County for Louisville site; Boone, Kenton, and Campbell Counties for Northern KY site)[1][2][5]

benefits.value: Primary and specialty medical care, prescription medications, adult day services, home care services, mental health and social services, skilled therapies (physical, occupational), nursing home care, hospital care, transportation to/from PACE center and medical visits, meals, nutritional assessments, socialization at PACE centers; coordinated by interdisciplinary team; becomes sole source of Medicare/Medicaid services upon enrollment[1][2][3][5][6]

methods: In-person: Louisville PACE Center at 960 S 4th St, Louisville KY 40203; Northern KY at 47 Cavalier Blvd., Suite 140, Florence, KY 41042[1][2][4]
Visit PACE center to inquire about eligibility[2]
forms: No specific form names/numbers identified; verify Medicaid eligibility via (800) 807-1301 or KYHealth-Net[5]
documents: Medicaid eligibility verification; documentation for nursing home level of care certification[5]

geography.restrictions: Limited to Jefferson County (Louisville) and Boone, Kenton, Campbell Counties (Northern KY)[1][2][5]
geography.regional_variations: Separate providers/centers: Senior CommUnity Care of Kentucky (Jefferson/Louisville) vs. Senior CommUnity Care of Northern Kentucky (Boone/Kenton/Campbell); different phone numbers, addresses, and emails[1][2][4][5]
offices_or_providers: Northern KY: 47 Cavalier Blvd., Suite 140, Florence, KY 41042; (859) 970-2450

gotchas: Not statewide—only specific counties; confirm residence[1][2][5]
Medicare-only or private pay participants face fees/restrictions, not 100% covered like dually eligible[3]
All non-emergency care must go through PACE providers to be covered[3]
Enrollment makes PACE the sole source of services—no duplication with other programs[5]
Must be able to live safely in community at enrollment time, despite nursing home level needs[1][2][3]
programs.data_shape_notes: County-restricted to Jefferson and Northern KY counties only; two regional providers with separate contacts; no income/asset dollar limits specified; fees for non-dually eligible; nursing home level of care certification required

programs.name: Senior Companion Program (Community Action of Southern Kentucky)
programs._raw:
I cannot provide the comprehensive JSON guide you've requested because the search results contain insufficient information about the specific program "Senior Companion Program (Community Action of Southern Kentucky)."

Here's what the search results reveal and what's missing:

## What I Can Confirm

**For Volunteers (not clients):**
- Age requirement: 55 or older[1]
- Income limit: 200% of federal poverty level[1]
- Minimum commitment: 5 hours per week (up to 40)[1]
- Stipend: $4.00/hour[1]
- Requirements: Pass road test, background check, drivers history check, fingerprinting, NSOR check (6-8 weeks), 4-6 hours online driver education, 20+ hours onboarding training[1]

**For Seniors Receiving Services:**
- Services include: companionship, light housework, errands, transportation, light meal preparation, respite care[2][4]
- Contact: Central Kentucky Community Action at (270) 734-3604 or (270) 402-7194[4]
- Address: 332 Hood Avenue, Lebanon, KY 40033[4]

## Critical Gaps

The search results do **not** provide:
- Specific income dollar amounts (only "200% of federal poverty level" without the actual figures)
- Asset limits or exemptions
- Eligibility requirements for seniors *receiving* services (age, income, asset limits)
- Online application URLs or specific form names
- Processing timelines for client applications
- Waitlist information
- Regional variations across Southern Kentucky
- Whether this is county-restricted or statewide
- Documents needed for application

**Recommendation:** Contact Central Kentucky Community Action directly at the phone numbers provided[4] to obtain the detailed, program-specific information needed for your guide. They can provide current income thresholds, application procedures, and regional details that aren't captured in these search results.

programs.name: Kentucky Family Caregiver Program (Grandparents Raising Grandchildren)

eligibility.income_limits: Household income at or below 150% of the federal poverty level (exact dollar amounts vary annually by federal guidelines and household size; not specified in sources for current year). Applies to financial assistance only; other services may have broader access.[1][4][6][7][9]
other_requirements: Primary caregiver for a grandchild related by blood, marriage, or adoption.
Grandchild is 18 years of age or younger.
Grandchild lives in the caregiver's home, and neither parent resides in the household.
Not receiving monthly Kinship Care payments.[1][4][6][9]

benefits.value: Wide range of services including matching with support groups, information about resources, assistance accessing services, counseling, and training. Eligible participants may receive a grant or voucher per grandchild per fiscal year for clothing, respite, school supplies, educational needs, required legal services, medical/dental services, and other approved expenses (specific dollar amounts not stated).[6][9]

methods: Contact local Area Agency on Aging and Independent Living (AAA). Examples: Pennyrile Area Development District (peadd.org/kentucky-family-caregiver[1]), KIPDA (kipda.org[2]).

Contact Cabinet for Health and Family Services, Department for Aging and Independent Living (chfs.ky.gov/agencies/dail/Pages/caregiversupport.aspx[9]). No specific phone, form name, or mail/in-person details in sources.
documents: Proof of relationship, residency, household income, grandchild's age, confirmation parents do not live in home, and no Kinship Care (inferred; not explicitly listed).

geography.regional_variations: Administered through local Area Agencies on Aging and Independent Living (e.g., Pennyrile[1], KIPDA region: Jefferson, Oldham, Henry, Trimble, Spencer, Shelby, Hardin[2]). Variations in local providers and potentially services/wait times.
offices_or_providers: Local Area Agencies on Aging and Independent Living (contact via chfs.ky.gov[9])

gotchas: Cannot receive benefits if already getting Kinship Care payments ('double dip' rule); evaluate which program is better.[1][4][6]
Parents cannot reside in the household, even if not primary caregivers.[1][6][9]
Income limit (150% FPL) applies strictly to financial assistance; services like support groups may be more accessible.[1][4][6]
Adopted grandparents may still qualify under certain conditions.[7]
programs.data_shape_notes: Administered statewide via local Area Agencies on Aging; financial aid requires 150% FPL income test and no Kinship Care overlap; benefits include non-cash services plus discretionary grants/vouchers per fiscal year.
